# react-zmage

> A React component that turns any `<img>` into a fullscreen-zoomable, multi-image, keyboard-navigable image viewer. Drop-in replacement for `<img>`. Supports React 16.8 through 19.

The package is published to npm as `react-zmage`. Default export is a `forwardRef` component; static methods on it provide imperative (`Zmage.browsing`) and HTML-wrapper (`Zmage.Wrapper`) usage modes. Styles are exported separately at `react-zmage/style.css`. SSR/RSC consumers use the `react-zmage/ssr` subpath.

## Quick start

```bash
pnpm add react-zmage   # or: npm install react-zmage / yarn add react-zmage
```

```tsx
import Zmage from 'react-zmage'
import 'react-zmage/style.css'

// Component mode (most common): replace any <img> with <Zmage>
<Zmage src="/photo.jpg" alt="hero" />

// Multi-image gallery: arrow keys flip pages
<Zmage src="/photo.jpg" set={[
  { src: '/photo.jpg', alt: 'one' },
  { src: '/photo.jpg', alt: 'two' },
]} />

// Imperative: open the viewer from any event handler. Returns a destructor.
// `opts` accepts the same fields as <Zmage> props (see API reference below).
const close = Zmage.browsing({ src: '/photo.jpg', backdrop: '#111' })
// later: close()

// Wrapper: auto-attach to every <img> inside an HTML subtree
<Zmage.Wrapper>
  <article dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: html }} />
</Zmage.Wrapper>

// Controlled mode: drive open/close from React state via the `browsing` PROP
// (not to be confused with the static method Zmage.browsing()). Always pair
// the prop with onBrowsing so external state stays in sync.
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
<button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Open</button>
<Zmage src="/photo.jpg" browsing={open} onBrowsing={setOpen} backdrop="#111" />

// SSR / RSC: import from the SSR-safe subpath (no browser globals at module load)
// import Zmage from 'react-zmage/ssr'
```

## Choosing a usage mode

- **Component**: you control the markup. Highest specificity, recommended default.
- **Imperative**: open from arbitrary callbacks (e.g., a button onClick handler outside JSX). `Zmage.browsing(opts)` returns a destructor ŌĆö calling it closes the viewer. Guard with `typeof window !== 'undefined'` if it can run server-side.
- **Wrapper**: you don't control the rendered HTML (CMS markup, MDX, `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`). Wrapper scans its `<img>` descendants on mount and on every re-render, then binds click handlers; new imgs injected without a Wrapper re-render are not picked up.

## API quick reference

`<Zmage>` props (and the same options bag accepts these for `Zmage.browsing(opts)`):

| Prop | Type | Default | Notes |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| `src` | `string` | ŌĆö | Required. Same as `<img src>`. |
| `alt` | `string` | `''` | Same as `<img alt>`. |
| `set` | `Array<{ src, alt? }>` | `[]` | Multi-image gallery. When non-empty, arrow keys page. |
| `defaultPage` | `number` | `0` | Initial index when `set` is non-empty. |
| `backdrop` | `string` (CSS color) | `'#FFFFFF'` | **Default is white** ŌĆö override (e.g. `'#111'`) for dark UIs or you'll get a white-on-white modal. |
| `preset` | `'desktop' \| 'mobile'` | `'desktop'` | Picks default `controller` / `hotKey` / `animate` bundle. |
| `controller` | `boolean \| ControllerSet` | preset | Per-button toggles: `pagination`, `rotate`, `rotateLeft`, `rotateRight`, `zoom`, `download`, `close`, `flip`, `flipLeft`, `flipRight`. |
| `hotKey` | `boolean \| { close?, zoom?, flip? }` | preset | Esc / Space / ŌåÉŌåÆ toggles. |
| `animate` | `boolean \| { flip?: 'fade' \| 'crossFade' \| 'swipe' \| 'zoom' }` | preset | Transition style between images. |
| `hideOnScroll` | `boolean` | `true` | Auto-close on page scroll (desktop only). |
| `coverVisible` | `boolean` | `false` | Keep the underlying `<img>` visible while the modal is open. |
| `zIndex` / `radius` / `edge` | `number` | `1000` / `0` / `0` | Modal stacking, image corner radius, viewport padding. |
| `loop` | `boolean` | `true` | Wrap-around when paging past the ends. |
| `onBrowsing` | `(isBrowsing: boolean) => void` | ŌĆö | Fires on open/close. |
| `onZooming` | `(isZooming: boolean) => void` | ŌĆö | Fires on zoom in/out. |
| `onSwitching` | `(page: number) => void` | ŌĆö | Fires on page change. |
| `onRotating` | `(deg: number) => void` | ŌĆö | Fires on rotation. |
| `browsing` | `boolean` | ŌĆö | **Controlled-mode prop**, distinct from the static method `Zmage.browsing()`. Pair with `onBrowsing` to drive open state from outside. |

Standard `<img>` HTML attributes (`className`, `style`, `width`, `height`, `loading`, `id`, etc.) pass through to the cover image.

## Theming

The library is theme-agnostic by design ŌĆö there's no theme provider, no light/dark switch. Read your resolved theme value at the host level and pass `backdrop` accordingly; icons render with SVG `currentColor`. Common host-side reset gotchas (Tailwind preflight, normalize.css, Bootstrap) were defended against in lib v1.1.2 ŌĆö upgrade if `<Zmage>` opens with the modal image rendering smaller than the cover.

## Docs

- [README.md](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/README.md): full human-facing documentation, install, all props, examples
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/AGENTS.md): condensed agent-oriented reference, public API contract, common pitfalls, architectural invariants
- [Online docs](https://zmage.caldis.me/docs): API reference, three-mode walkthrough, theming guide, FAQ

## Source of truth

- [packages/core/src/types/global.ts](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/packages/core/src/types/global.ts): canonical TypeScript definitions for all props (`BaseType`, `Set`, `Preset`, `ControllerSet`, `HotKey`, `Animate`, lifecycle params)
- [packages/core/src/types/default.ts](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/packages/core/src/types/default.ts): canonical defaults (`defProp`, `defPreset` for desktop/mobile)
- [packages/core/src/index.ts](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/packages/core/src/index.ts): runtime exports
- [packages/core/package.json](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/packages/core/package.json): publish contract (`exports` field, peer deps)

## Examples

- [packages/home/src/App.tsx](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/packages/home/src/App.tsx): live demo site source
- [packages/sandbox-r17/src/consumer.tsx](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage/blob/master/packages/sandbox-r17/src/consumer.tsx): minimal R17 consumer (also r18, r19)
- [Live playground](https://zmage.caldis.me/playground): real-time prop debugging

## Common pitfalls

1. Forgetting `import 'react-zmage/style.css'` ŌĆö viewer renders unstyled.
2. Confusing the static method `Zmage.browsing(opts)` (imperative open, returns a destructor) with the `browsing` prop (controlled-mode boolean). Same name, different things ŌĆö the prop must always be paired with an `onBrowsing` handler or state desyncs silently.
3. Wrapper not picking up dynamically-injected imgs ŌĆö Wrapper only re-scans on its own re-render, not on DOM mutations.
4. Calling `Zmage.browsing()` in code that may run server-side ŌĆö guard with `typeof window !== 'undefined'`, or import from `react-zmage/ssr`.
5. Leaving `backdrop` at the default `#FFFFFF` on a dark-themed page ŌĆö the modal will render white-on-white. Always set `backdrop` explicitly if your host UI isn't light.

## Optional

- [Online demo](https://zmage.caldis.me)
- [GitHub repo](https://github.com/Caldis/react-zmage)
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-zmage)
